nodes.name: Receive Firecrawl Request
nodes.notes: Receives { source: 'firecrawl', query, max_results, include_domains, exclude_domains, language } from the CRM's Scrape Leads dialog. If you already have the main /webhook/3f8ea5dc-... scrape router, you can either: (A) wire its Switch node to route source=='firecrawl' here, OR (B) point the CRM dialog directly at THIS workflow's webhook URL by editing SCRAPE_WEBHOOK_URL in Frontend/src/components/modules/ScrapeLeadsDialog.tsx.

nodes.name: Firecrawl /search
nodes.notes: Calls Firecrawl /search to discover relevant company pages. Returns up to N results with full page markdown so we can extract. Costs 2 credits per 10 results.

nodes.name: Split Each Result URL
nodes.notes: Each search result becomes its own n8n item. The Extract node below runs once per URL.

nodes.name: Firecrawl /extract
nodes.notes: LLM-powered structured extraction per URL. Costs ~1 credit per page (sometimes more if Firecrawl needs to crawl sub-pages to fulfill the schema). On free tier (1k credits/mo), 10-result search → ~$0.10 worth of credits used.

string.name: notes

nodes.name: Has Any Contact?
nodes.notes: Permissive gate — pass any row that has AT LEAST ONE of company/name/email/phone. Backend fills missing name/company by falling back to each other. Phone-only and social-only leads still come through so the team can reach out manually; the sequence-enrollment picker then filters down to email-eligible leads at send time.

nodes.name: Ingest into Seekers CRM
nodes.notes: Hits /outreach/leads/ingest. Idempotent by email — if Firecrawl returns a company you already have, the existing lead is patched, not duplicated.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { ok: true, message: 'Firecrawl search dispatched. Results will arrive over the next 1-3 min.' } }}

nodes.name: Respond OK to CRM
nodes.notes: Responds immediately to the CRM's dialog so the user gets a fast toast. The actual extraction + ingestion runs in the background per item.
